compiled_state.title: Update project docs to reflect two-channel release model
compiled_state.author: Claude
compiled_state.description:
## Description

**What**: Update existing project documentation to reflect the two-channel release model: CLAUDE.md (architecture/quick reference sections), an ADR capturing the decision rationale, and KNOWN-ISSUES.md (note the validate-issues OSError 75 from bug 3cd9-5a95 in case it recurs in other web sessions).

**Why**: After the cutover, internal contributors and future agents need accurate awareness of the new release process and the rationale behind it. Without this, the next maintainer (human or agent) will not know the channel/tag conventions or where the release tooling lives.

**Scope**:
- IN: Update CLAUDE.md (one-liner in Architecture section + new Quick Reference table row for scripts/release.sh); add docs/adr/00X-plugin-release-channels.md following the ADR template style of design-notes.md ADR-001; add KNOWN-ISSUES.md entry for the validate-issues OSError 75 (cross-link to bug 3cd9-5a95).
- OUT: INSTALL.md (already in Story 2 scope); creating new doc patterns or files beyond the ADR; rewriting plugin source documentation.

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, CLAUDE.md mentions the two-channel release model in the Architecture section and lists scripts/release.sh in the Quick Reference table.
- When this story is complete, docs/adr/00X-plugin-release-channels.md exists and captures: Status (Accepted), Context (problem statement), Decision (two-channel pattern with stable=tag/dev=main), Consequences (release process, version bump constraint, client migration).
- When this story is complete, KNOWN-ISSUES.md has an entry for validate-issues.sh OSError 75 in the Claude Code web environment, cross-linking bug 3cd9-5a95.

## Considerations

- [Maintainability] Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for formatting and structure.
- [Maintainability] ADR numbering: increment from existing ADRs in design-notes.md (ADR-001 currently exists).

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Proceed unless a significant assumption is required to continue — one that could send the implementation in the wrong direction. Escalate only when genuinely blocked without a reasonable inference. Document all assumptions made without escalating.

comments.body: Follow .claude/docs/DOCUMENTATION-GUIDE.md for documentation formatting, structure, and conventions.
comments.author: Claude
